q3: The biggest thing was that opening smell description — it started too generic, like a menu listing ingredients rather than an actual physical experience, and I had to basically hand it the specific detail myself before it clicked. Once I gave it the cedar-sauna thing it ran with it beautifully, but I wish it had pushed for that level of specificity on its own rather than waiting for me to diagnose the problem and supply the fix. The pacing instincts got sharper as we went, which I appreciated.
